skill.name: referral-letter-generator
skill.description: Generate professional medical referral letters for patient transfers between healthcare providers. Accepts patient data including diagnosis, medical history, and medications, then outputs formatted letters in PDF, DOCX, HTML, or TXT format.
skill.summary: A tool for generating structured medical referral letters with patient summary, reason for referral, and provider information.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Security evaluation of 13 files (2104 lines) shows the skill is a legitimate medical referral letter generator. All 30 detected 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives caused by medical reference text containing ICD-10 codes, medication dosages, and template variable patterns. All 97 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives from Markdown code blocks in documentation files. The only confirmed risk is minor: os.system() usage for auto-installing Python dependencies (reportlab, python-docx) at scripts/main.py:355 and scripts/main.py:473. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or credential theft was found.

low_findings.title: Auto-install of Python dependencies via os.system
low_findings.description: The script calls os.system() to auto-install reportlab (line 355) and python-docx (line 473) when they are not already installed. While commands are hardcoded strings with no user input, this is an anti-pattern that silently modifies the execution environment without user confirmation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The os.system calls use hardcoded pip install commands with sys.executable, making injection unlikely. However, auto-installing dependencies at runtime without confirmation is a questionable pattern.

content.user_title: Generate Medical Referral Letters with Patient Summaries
content.value_statement: Healthcare providers spend significant time writing referral letters manually, which can lead to inconsistent formatting and missing critical information. This skill automates the creation of professional, structured referral letters with all required patient data, diagnosis details, and provider information in one step.
actual_capabilities: Generates referral letters in four output formats: PDF, DOCX, HTML, and plain text
Validates required fields including patient name, provider names, diagnosis, and referral reason
Includes optional sections for medical history, medications, allergies, vital signs, and lab results
Supports urgency levels: Routine, Urgent, and Emergent with appropriate formatting
Loads patient data from structured JSON input for batch processing
Produces sample referral data for testing and demonstration purposes
limitations: Does not integrate with electronic health record (EHR) systems for direct data import
Cannot verify clinical accuracy or completeness of the medical content provided
Does not support encrypted transmission or secure messaging between providers
Requires manual installation of PDF and DOCX libraries if they are missing from the environment

use_cases.title: Primary Care to Specialist Referral
use_cases.description: A primary care physician prepares a referral letter for a patient with suspected coronary artery disease who needs to see a cardiologist. The skill formats patient history, medications, stress test results, and urgency level into a professional PDF letter.

use_cases.title: Hospital Discharge and Care Transfer
use_cases.description: A hospitalist transfers a patient to a skilled nursing facility for continued care. The skill generates a comprehensive referral letter including discharge diagnosis, pending lab results, medication changes, and follow-up requirements.

use_cases.title: Urgent Referral for Specialty Consultation
use_cases.description: An emergency department physician needs an urgent referral for a patient with acute neurological symptoms. The skill creates a clearly marked urgent referral containing key clinical findings, imaging results, and the requested consultation timeframe.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Referral Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a medical referral letter for [PATIENT_NAME], a [AGE]-year-old patient with [DIAGNOSIS], being referred to [SPECIALIST_NAME] in [SPECIALTY]. Include the reason for referral, relevant medical history, and current medications in the output.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user creating a simple referral letter with basic patient information

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Referral with Clinical Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive referral letter for patient [NAME] (DOB: [DOB], MRN: [MRN]) from Dr. [REFERRING] to Dr. [RECEIVING] in [DEPARTMENT]. Include primary diagnosis of [DIAGNOSIS], full medical history, current medications list [MEDS], known allergies [ALLERGIES], recent lab results [LABS], and vital signs [VITALS]. Set urgency to [URGENCY_LEVEL].
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user generating a detailed referral with complete clinical data

prompt_templates.title: Batch Referral Format Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the referral letter generator, process the patient data from [INPUT_JSON_PATH] and produce a [PDF|DOCX|HTML|TXT] referral letter saved to [OUTPUT_PATH]. Validate all required fields are present before generating and report any missing fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user generating referrals from structured JSON input files

prompt_templates.title: Custom Urgent Referral with Follow-up Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an urgent referral letter for a [AGE]-year-old [GENDER] patient with [SYMPTOMS]. Set urgency to Urgent. Include the referring provider contact information. Add a note requesting that the specialist call after evaluation. Output the letter as PDF with patient data auto-generated from the following JSON: [INSERT_JSON].
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user creating time-sensitive urgent referrals with specific post-referral instructions

output_examples.input: Create a referral letter for Jane Smith, a 38-year-old female with suspected coronary artery disease, being referred to Dr. Sarah Williams in Cardiology. Urgency: Urgent.
output_examples.output: A formatted PDF document titled MEDICAL REFERRAL LETTER with a centered header, date, and red URGENCY banner. The letter contains a two-column TO/FROM provider section with names and titles, a patient information block with name, DOB, and MRN, followed by sections for reason for referral, primary diagnosis, relevant medical history (hypertension, diabetes), current medications (lisinopril, metformin, atorvastatin, aspirin), and allergies. The footer includes a thank-you note and the referring provider signature.

output_examples.input: Generate a sample referral letter for testing purposes and save as DOCX format.
output_examples.output: A Word document (.docx) containing a sample referral letter for Jane Smith with pre-populated sample data including City General Hospital as the referring institution, Heart Care Center as the destination, and complete clinical details including vital signs, lab results, and medication lists.

best_practices: Always validate all required input fields (patient name, DOB, patient ID, diagnosis, provider names) before running the generator to avoid incomplete letters
Use the --sample flag first for testing to verify output formatting and then replace with real patient data once the format is confirmed
Review generated letters for clinical accuracy and completeness before sending, as the tool formats provided data but does not verify medical correctness
anti_patterns: Do not include insurance billing codes or financial information in the referral data, as the letter format is designed for clinical communication only
Do not bypass input validation by providing empty strings for required fields, as this produces incomplete letters that lack essential patient identification
Do not use the auto-install capability in restricted or air-gapped environments without pre-installing the required reportlab and python-docx packages manually

faq.question: What output formats does the referral letter generator support?
faq.answer: The generator supports four formats: PDF (professional distribution), DOCX (Word editing), HTML (web viewing), and TXT (plain text preview).

faq.question: Is patient data sent over the internet when generating referral letters?
faq.answer: No. All patient data is processed locally on your machine. The script reads input from local files and writes output to local files with no network calls.

faq.question: What are the required input fields for a referral letter?
faq.answer: Required fields are patient name, date of birth, patient ID, reason for referral, primary diagnosis, referring provider name, and receiving provider name.

faq.question: Can I add lab results and vital signs to the referral letter?
faq.answer: Yes. The generator supports optional sections for lab results, vital signs, medical history, current medications, allergies, and additional notes.

faq.question: Does this tool integrate with electronic health record systems?
faq.answer: No. The tool works with standalone JSON input files and produces output documents. It does not connect to any EHR system or patient database.

faq.question: What Python dependencies are required to run this tool?
faq.answer: The tool requires Python 3.8 or later and the reportlab library for PDF output and python-docx for DOCX output. These libraries are auto-installed if missing, but pre-installation is recommended.
